channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II crew will endure 3,000°C on re-entry. A hypersonics expert explains how they will survive - The Conversation
item.description:
1. Artemis II crew will endure 3,000°C on re-entry. A hypersonics expert explains how they will survive  The Conversation
  2. Artemis II astronauts reflect on record journey before return  Yahoo
  3. Solar Eclipse of the Heart  NASA (.gov)
  4. NASA Artemis II astronauts prepare to end moon mission in 'fireball' re-entry  Reuters
  5. An Incredibly Weird Time to Be Alive  The Atlantic

item.title: Artemis II relied on European science: what that means for the region’s space ambitions - Nature
item.description: Artemis II relied on European science: what that means for the region’s space ambitions  NatureView Full Coverage on Google News

item.title: How Artemis II’s Earthset photo compares with the iconic Earthrise image from 1968 - The Conversation
item.description:
1. How Artemis II’s Earthset photo compares with the iconic Earthrise image from 1968  The Conversation
  2. Earthrise to Earthset: how the planet’s climate has changed since the photo that inspired the environmental movement  The Conversation
  3. Why do Earth's colors appear muted in new photos from Artemis II?  KSBW
  4. New NASA photo promises to shut down flat-Earthers — did it work?  Yahoo
  5. 58 Years After ‘Earthrise,’ NASA’s New Moonshot May Rediscover Earth  The New York Times

item.title: Daylight fireball seen from New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania - The Watchers - Watching the world evolve and transform
item.description:
1. Daylight fireball seen from New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania  The Watchers - Watching the world evolve and transform
  2. Another day, another meteor. 5 states report fireball sighting.  USA Today
  3. NASA confirms fireball seen over Philly region skies was a meteor on Tuesday  NBC10 Philadelphia
  4. Fireball spotted in Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Delaware traveled at 30,000 mph before disintegrating, NASA says  CBS News
  5. Giant fireball spotted streaking across Mid-Atlantic states  New York Post
item.source: The Watchers - Watching the world evolve and transform

item.title: Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife - NPR
item.description:
1. Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife  NPR
  2. Artemis II crew tearfully proposes to name moon crater after astronaut Reid Wiseman's late wife  NBC News
  3. Artemis II astronauts reflect on historic flight as they wrap up moon mission  CBS News
  4. Astronauts Dedicate Moon Crater to Carroll Wiseman, Wife of NASA Commander  The New York Times
  5. Moon memorial: Artemis 2 astronauts name lunar 'bright spot' after mission commander's late wife  Space

item.title: ‘Non-survivable’: heatwaves are already breaching human limits, with worse to come, study finds - The Guardian
item.description:
1. ‘Non-survivable’: heatwaves are already breaching human limits, with worse to come, study finds  The Guardian
  2. Deadly 119°F Heatwaves are 'Already Here': Why They Are Now Labeled ‘Non-Survivable’ for 2026  Yahoo
  3. Deadly heat stress conditions already occurring  The Australian National University
  4. Non-survivable heatwaves: What scientists just learned about extreme temperatures and wet-bulb temperature threshold  The Times of India
  5. Global warming already impacts daily lives around the globe, study finds  Mongabay

item.title: Museum display found to be entirely new species after 26 years: 'Difficult to decipher' - Yahoo News Australia
item.description:
1. Museum display found to be entirely new species after 26 years: 'Difficult to decipher'  Yahoo News Australia
  2. Earliest known octopus is not an octopus after all  BBC
  3. Scientists say the world's oldest octopus fossil isn't an octopus after all  ClickOnDetroit | WDIV Local 4
  4. Scientists Baffled After World Guinness ‘Oldest’ Octopus Turns Out To Not Be An Octopus At All  AOL.com
  5. This Fossil Once Rewrote Everything We Knew About Octopuses. Turns Out, It's Not An Octopus At All  IFLScience

item.title: Christian Artemis II pilot Victor Glover reflects on God's creation from space - Christian Post
item.description:
1. Christian Artemis II pilot Victor Glover reflects on God's creation from space  Christian Post

  4. Sermon on the Integrity: Artemis II mission pilot preaches Christ's command to love on lunar flyby  OSV News

item.title: Is the next 'great comet' coming? See Comet PanSTARRS brighten in the night sky this week. - Live Science
item.description:

item.title: What Is Going On With the Artemis 2 Toilet? - Gizmodo
item.description:

  4. See the Artemis II astronauts give a tour of their ride to the moon  National Geographic

item.title: Japan team discovers why cats leave meals unfinished - The Japan Times
item.description:
1. Japan team discovers why cats leave meals unfinished  The Japan Times
  2. Scientists Think They’ve Finally Discovered Why Your Cat Never Finishes Its Meal  Gizmodo
  3. Why cats stop eating even though they’re still hungry  Earth.com
  4. Does Your Cat Never Finish Its Meals? It’s Probably Bored Of The Food You’re Serving  IFLScience
  5. The reason why cats leave food uneaten has been explained: they will eat it again if the smell of the food changes.  GIGAZINE
